how_it_works: Atlas maps are tiered 1–16. Tiers 1–5 are white (normal), 6–10 are yellow (magic/rare), and 11–16 are red. Higher tiers give more experience, better item quantity/rarity, and access to endgame mechanics. The Atlas Passive Tree lets you specialize regions to amplify specific league mechanics or boss encounters.
tips: Sustain red maps by targeting maps you can reliably complete with high pack size — don't spread too thin across all tiers.
Voidstone from Pinnacle bosses (Sirus, Maven, Uber Pinnacles) raises your Atlas level, shifting all maps to higher base tiers.
Run Sextants (Charged Compasses) on Watchstones for highest-value encounters; prioritize Beyond, Delirium, and Ritual.

tiers.rarity_requirement: Normal maps can be cleared as-is; magic/rare for bonus objectives
tiers.atlas_objective: Complete white map bonus objectives for Atlas passive points
tiers.notes: White maps provide minimal drops but are required to unlock yellow maps. Focus on completing them quickly rather than farming them.

tiers.rarity_requirement: Run as magic or rare for best yield; alch and go is standard
key_mechanics: Starting league mechanics (Delirium mirrors, Ritual altars)
tiers.atlas_objective: Complete yellow map bonus objectives; unlock red map access
tiers.notes: The first real farming tier. Alch-and-go T8–10 maps is the standard progression entry point. Prioritize completing all map types for atlas points.

tiers.rarity_requirement: Always run as rare; Sextants recommended; Scarabs add significant value
key_mechanics: Map device recipes (sacrifice frags, Zana mods)
tiers.atlas_objective: Progress toward Shaper/Elder guardians; unlock T15–16
tiers.notes: Entry point for serious farming. Chisel + alch + vaal is standard. Sextants become cost-effective at this tier.

tiers.rarity_requirement: Chisel + alch + vaal; Sextants; Scarabs; Prophecies (if active)
key_mechanics: Grand Design / Full Atlas passive synergies
tiers.atlas_objective: Farm Voidstones from Sirus, Maven, and 4 Pinnacle bosses; maximum atlas completion
tiers.notes: Full 4-Voidstone setup enables T16 as baseline. The highest farming tier. Max Sextants, Scarabs, and Atlas passives stack multiplicatively here.

tiers.rarity_requirement: Run as-is (unique maps cannot be corrupted for quality normally)
key_mechanics: Chase uniques (Headhunter from Maelström of Chaos, etc.)
tiers.atlas_objective: Atlas completion bonus; chase specific unique drops
tiers.notes: Some unique maps (Poorjoy's, Maelström) are specifically farmed for their chase uniques. Run them when the target item is worth the investment.

key_mechanics: Uber Pinnacles (Uber fragments from Sanctum, Expedition, Ritual)
tiers.atlas_objective: Kill all pinnacle bosses; collect Voidstones; target exclusive drop tables
notable_maps: The Searing Exarch / Eater of Worlds (Uber versions)
tiers.notes: Pinnacle bosses drop mirror-tier uniques (Mageblood, Headhunter) and Voidstones. Required for full atlas completion and T16 baseline.

key_mechanics: Specialize one region's atlas passives for dominant strategy
Stack Delirium, Beyond, Harvest, or Ritual depending on build/league
Sextant value multiplied in your chosen region
tiers.atlas_objective: Maximize the value of a single farming strategy rather than spreading passives thin
tiers.notes: The Atlas Passive Tree has ~600 points. Focus one region first, complete all maps in it for points, then branch out. Generalist tree is weaker than a focused strategy.
